question: Will Kite Robotics and Halvorsen Motors sign a component-supply partnership before the fall production freeze?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

production_freeze_timing.description: Production freeze occurs approximately 8 weeks from prediction date (2025-07-18), i.e., around 2025-09-12

offer_exclusivity.description: Kite Robotics can offer either 12% volume discount OR co-marketing clause, not both

committee_decision_visibility.rule: decision_record is accessible to all committee members and CEO after recording

proposal_terms_known.rule: proposal terms (discount, co-marketing) are known to committee before vote

amara_diallo.why_consequential: Decides which offer terms (discount or co-marketing) to propose; submits proposal to Halvorsen

erik_halvorsen.why_consequential: Delegated supplier onboarding to committee; can override committee decision or influence vote

sofia_brandt.why_consequential: Chairs committee meetings; records decisions; casts vote

unresolved_mechanisms: Exact committee membership beyond chair (Sofia Brandt) and CEO (Erik Halvorsen) is unspecified; assumed majority requires both to approve
Whether Erik Halvorsen retains veto power after delegation is not specified
Exact date of next committee meeting relative to proposal submission is unknown
assumptions: Committee meets at least once between 2025-07-18 and 2025-09-30
Kite Robotics submits a proposal before the production freeze
Committee decision requires majority of voting members
Production freeze date is exactly 8 weeks from prediction date (2025-09-12)

auto_repairs: institution 'halvorsen_procurement_committee' aggregation defaulted to majority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No explicit representation of the 'fall production freeze' date or season — the freeze is modeled as a generic event with no calendar anchor, so the temporal comparison 'before fall freeze' is undefined
No entity or attribute for 'Kite Robotics' as a company — the model has actors like Amara Diallo but no company entity for Kite Robotics to link to
No fallback or escalation path if committee rejects — the process ends at 'rejected' with no re-submission or override affordance modeled as a transition
No representation of external market conditions, supply chain disruptions, or regulatory approvals that could delay signing
relabeled_progress_bars: 'under_committee_review' is a black-box state — no sub-states for 'reviewing terms', 'legal review', 'price negotiation'
'committee_decision_made' conflates vote outcome with final decision — no separate 'pending ratification' or 'executive sign-off' step
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes committee majority vote is the sole decision mechanism, but Erik Halvorsen's override affordance is not wired into any transition condition
The 'time_elapsed' trigger for freeze is vague — no mechanism to know if freeze date is fixed or flexible, making 'before freeze' comparison unreliable
No representation of whether the proposal submission deadline relative to committee meeting schedule matters — monthly meetings could cause missed windows
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Amara Diallo's affordances list 'negotiate_terms_with_committee' but no corresponding event type or transition for negotiation — it's a dangling affordance
Sofia Brandt's 'recommend_approval_or_rejection' affordance has no corresponding fact or event — it's not linked to any vote or decision record
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'committee_vote' as transparent and recorded, but does not model informal pre-meeting lobbying or private side deals that could sway votes
The 'co_marketing_clause_included' field in partnership is binary but real negotiations often involve conditional or phased co-marketing — model oversimplifies
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No calendar or clock mechanism to compare signature_date to freeze_date — the predicate only checks existence, not temporal ordering
No mechanism for automated procurement systems or ERP deadlines that might force a freeze regardless of human decisions
No representation of material availability, logistics lead times, or quality audits that are prerequisite to signing
